purpose: An empty-state illustration used inside IllustratedMessage when the user needs to add dimensions to a chart, pivot table, or analytical view. Signals 'no data because no dimensions selected yet — pick some to start'.
whenToUse: Empty analytical chart before any dimensions are selected
Pivot table with no row/column dimensions
Report builder with no data axes configured
Any analytics view awaiting user configuration
whenNotToUse: For 'no data returned' after a query — use different illustration
For a general empty list — use appropriate empty-state variant
As a decorative element — always paired with actionable text
doRules: Pair the illustration with clear title and description
Provide a call-to-action button ('Add dimensions', 'Configure chart')
Use the appropriate size (Spot, Dialog, Scene) for the container
Keep description short — the illustration does the visual work
dontRules: Do not use in non-empty states
Do not omit the CTA — user needs a path forward
Do not shrink below Spot size (48×48) — becomes illegible
Do not use if the actual empty state has a different cause

layoutGuidance.placement: Inside IllustratedMessage container in empty chart/table region
layoutGuidance.alignment: Centered vertically and horizontally in the empty region

contentGuidance.labelLength: Title ≤ 40 chars; description ≤ 120 chars; button ≤ 20 chars
contentGuidance.contentRules: Title states the empty state; description explains; button is action-first
examples: Title: 'No dimensions selected' · Description: 'Add dimensions to start analyzing' · Button: 'Add dimensions'

requirements: Illustration has role='img' with meaningful aria-label
CTA button clearly describes the action
Focus moves to CTA when illustration is shown
